text: Strong.  It is broadly accepted that pro-proliferative signaling is activated in an attempt to compensate for increases in cell death (Stanger 2015). Increased hepatocyte proliferation on a background of polyploidy, elevated cell death and oxidative DNA damage has the effect of increasing the likelihood of fixing harmful mutations, which are necessary for malignant transformation (Celton-Morizur and Desdouets 2010, Shi and Line 2014).  However, the precise mechanistic processes defining this relationship have not been mapped out.
